text: The surgeon begins with sigmoid colon mobilization, facilitating left ureterolysis and freeing the bowel. The ovaries are mobilized, revealing small endometriomas, which are excised from both ovaries. Ovarian suspension is performed to ensure complete exposure.

The uterus is visibly suspended, providing clear access to the surgical field for the excision of deep infiltrative endometriosis. The ovaries are also positioned out of the way, allowing the assistant to actively engage in the procedure.

The surgeon uses cold scissors to separate the bowel from the back of the uterus, entering the rectovaginal space. Fat from the parorectal space becomes visible during the dissection. Blunt and sharp dissection techniques are employed to separate the structures, while ureterolysis continues on the left side, revealing the left ureter.
The surgeon carefully navigates around the left pararectal space, avoiding the hypogastric nerves, and proceeds to free up the bowel by dissecting around it.

The surgeon skeletonizes important structures and removes abnormal tissue from the utero-sacral ligament and parorectal space. The right parietal space is opened, and the segmental bowel resection enters the holy plane, located behind the rectum and anterior to the hypogastric fascia. A full thickness rectal nodule is visible.

The surgeon completes the mobilization of the bowel, ensuring the operating field is clean and tidy. Dissection continues along visible tissue planes, with all visible endometriosis carefully excised to remove any remnants.

The surgeon cleans the bowel and identifies the site for stapler application. Mr. Nisar removes the bowel segment and brings out the proximal end for segmental resection. The anvil is placed, and the anastomosis is performed.
